stem: Lines p, q, and r form a triangle. One interior angle of the triangle is vertical to an angle of measure a°. A second interior angle is supplementary to an angle of measure b°. The third interior angle is bisected by another line into two equal sub-angles, each of measure c°. If a = 51 and b = 115, what is the value of c?
rationale: The interior angle vertical to a° has measure 51°. The interior angle supplementary to b° has measure 180 − 115 = 65°. The three interior angles of the triangle sum to 180°, so the third interior angle measures 180 − 51 − 65 = 64°. That angle is bisected into two equal sub-angles of measure c°, so 2c = 64, giving c = 32.
